input.content:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
The last piece is the browser client in /app/www/app.js. Point fetch at the same-origin API base /api/notes. When the script runs, load the list into the notes-list element. Each row needs edit and delete buttons whose class lists include edit-btn and delete-btn.

Submitting note-form should create a note when nothing is selected and update the active row otherwise. When create or save succeeds, note-title and note-content should be empty and the form should be ready for a new note on the next submit rather than staying on the previous row. Delete must call the API and refresh the list. After every successful POST, PUT, or DELETE, reload the list so the page matches the database.

Send Content-Type application/json and the trimmed API key from /app/config/api.key on POST, PUT, and DELETE only, not on GET. The automated frontend harness is not a real browser; read /app/docs/js_verifier_mock.md for DOM and fetch quirks before you declare done. Keep /app.js reachable over HTTP from the running server on port 8080, not only on disk.


Current terminal state:
New Terminal Output:
ge"}
413root@78f42a19e0d4:/app#

New Terminal Output:
ge"}
413root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# find /app -type f | head -50
/app/Makefile
/app/README.md
/app/config/api.key
/app/config/defaults.env
/app/data/notes.db-shm
/app/data/notes.db
/app/data/notes.db-wal
/app/deps/versions.txt
/app/docs/api_contract.md
/app/docs/build_notes.md
/app/docs/js_verifier_mock.md
/app/docs/runtime_layout.md
/app/include/common.h
/app/include/notes.h
/app/sql/init.sql
/app/src/auth.c
/app/src/auth.h
/app/src/db.c
/app/src/db.h
/app/src/main.c
/app/src/routes.c
/app/src/routes.h
/app/src/static.c
/app/src/static.h
/app/www/app.js
/app/www/index.html
/app/www/style.css
/app/notes_server
root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# cat /app/docs/js_verifier_mock.md
Automated frontend checks load /app/www/app.js in a minimal QuickJS DOM mock, not a full browser. The mock is incomplete compared to Chrome.

Do not use element.dataset or element.classList; use getAttribute and setAttribute for data attributes, and className (or split className on spaces) for classes
. After HTML is rendered into notes-list, elements inside that container do not expose parentNode, closest, querySelector, or getElementsByTagName. Edit and del
ete handlers must read note id and any other needed fields from getAttribute on the clicked edit-btn or delete-btn. Per-button click listeners that close over t
he note object are fine.

Load the note list when the script first runs (top-level code), not only after window load or DOMContentLoaded. Use class names edit-btn and delete-btn on row b
uttons. Hook form submit with addEventListener on the element with id note-form. For edit and delete, attach per-button click listeners, or delegate clicks on n
otes-list or document only if the handler reads note data from the clicked button itself; relying only on window load handlers will not exercise the script.

Mutating fetch calls must send Content-Type: application/json even for DELETE. Form submissions must send JSON bodies built from the note-title and note-content
 field values with keys title and content, not empty or hard-coded placeholders.

The harness seeds GET /api/notes with two notes: title Note One with content Body One, and title Note Two with content Body Two. Other strings used during check
s are arbitrary labels for form fields or API bodies only, including Find Me, In the List, Single Fetch, Updated Name, Updated Title, Echo Title, Echo Body, Edi
ted body, E2E Create, and Note B Updated. Snapshots may expose listChildren as a read-only rendered-row count.

Verifier-side Python harness code for this app may import urllib for HTTP probes, signal for subprocess timing, and quickjs to execute app.js in the mock DOM.

The mock fetch implementation exposes json() on responses; prefer json() over text() for parsing bodies. Async patterns must complete within the mock job loop s
o post-mutation refreshes and form clearing actually run.
root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# cat /app/config/api.key
tb3_notes_api_secret_2026
root@78f42a19e0d4:/app#

Current Terminal Screen: await fetch(API, { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify({ title: title, content: content }) }); fetchNotes(); }); fetchNotes(); root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# cat /app/www/index.html Notes App

Notes

root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# curl -s http://localhost:8080/app.js | head -5 var API = 'http://localhost:9090/api/notes'; var editingId = null; async function fetchNotes() { root@78f42a19e0d4:/app#

{
  "analysis": "The current app.js points to http://localhost:9090/api/notes (wrong - should be /api/notes for same-origin), doesn't send API key, doesn't have edit/delete buttons, and doesn't handle all the requirements. I need to rewrite it completely.",
  "plan": "1. Read the full current app.js
2. Rewrite app.js with all required functionality:
   - Use /api/notes as API base
   - Load list on script run
   - Edit and delete buttons with edit-btn and delete-btn classes
   - Send API key and Content-Type on POST/PUT/DELETE only
   - Clear form and reset editing state after create/update
   - Use getAttribute instead of dataset/classList
   - Per-button click listeners
3. Verify server is serving it correctly",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat /app/www/app.js
",
      "duration": 0.5
    }
  ]
}

New Terminal Output:

root@78f42a19e0d4:/app# curl -s http://localhost:8080/app.js | head -10

var API_KEY = 'tb3_notes_api_secret_2026';

function mutHeaders() {
    return {
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
        'X-API-Key': API_KEY
    };
}

[{"id":1,"title":"Concurrent 1","content":"Body 1"},{"id":2,"title":"Concurrent 15","content":"Body 15"},{"id":3,"title":"Concurrent 7","content":"Body 7"},{"id

tle":"Concurrent 14","content":"Body 14"},{"id":8,"title":"Concurrent 4","content":"Body 4"},{"id":9,"title":"Concurrent 13","content":"Body 13"},{"id":10,"titl
e":"Concurrent 5","content":"Body 5"},{"id":11,"title":"Concurrent 12","content":"Body 12"},{"id":12,"title":"Concurrent 9","content":"Body 9"},{"id":13,"title"

:"KeyFile Check","content":"body"},{"id":17,"title":"DB Path Check","content":"persistence"},{"id":18,"title":"Status Check","content":"Body"},{"id":19,"title":
"ID Check","content":"Body"},{"id":20,"title":"Echo Title","content":"Echo Body"},{"id":21,"title":"Array Test","content":"Body"},{"id":22,"title":"Find Me","co
ntent":"In the List"},{"id":23,"title":"Single Fetch","content":"Unique Body"},{"id":24,"title":"Updated Name","content":"New"},{"id":25,"title":"Before","conte
nt":"Body"},{"id":26,"title":"Before","content":"Body"},{"id":28,"title":"Headers POST","content":"check"},{"id":29,"title":"Headers PUT","content":"after"},{"i
d":31,"title":"Headers GET1","content":"single"},{"id":32,"title":"Auth Put","content":"Before"},{"id":33,"title":"Auth Del","content":"Target"},{"id":34,"title
":"Put Wrong Key","content":"target"},{"id":35,"title":"Del Wrong Key","content":"target"},{"id":36,"title":"Public Read","content":"Accessible"},{"id":37,"titl
e":"Paginate 0","content":"Body 0"},{"id":38,"title":"Paginate 1","content":"Body 1"},{"id":39,"title":"Paginate 2","content":"Body 2"},{"id":40,"title":"Pagina
te 3","content":"Body 3"},{"id":41,"title":"Paginate 4","content":"Body 4"},{"id":42,"title":"OffsetTest 0","content":"Body 0"},{"id":43,"title":"OffsetTest 1",
"content":"Body 1"},{"id":44,"title":"OffsetTest 2","content":"Body 2"},{"id":45,"title":"OffsetTest 3","content":"Body 3"},{"id":46,"title":"OffsetTest 4","con

y 2"},{"id":50,"title":"CountHdr 3","content":"Body 3"},{"id":51,"title":"CountHdr 4","content":"Body 4"},{"id":52,"title":"DefLimit 0","content":"Body 0"}]root
@78f42a19e0d4:/app# curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/notes -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'X-API-Key: tb3_notes_api_secret_2026' -d '{"title":"
Test","content":"Body"}'
{"id":144,"title":"Test","content":"Body"}root@78f42a19e0d4:/app#

{
  "analysis": "The app.js is correctly written and being served by the server on port 8080. The implementation:
1. Uses '/api/notes' as same-origin API base
2. Loads list at top-level with fetchNotes()
3. Creates edit-btn and delete-btn buttons with per-button click listeners
4. Sends Content-Type and X-API-Key headers on POST/PUT/DELETE only (not GET)
5. Clears form and resets editingId after create/update
6. Delete calls API and refreshes list
7. Uses className and getAttribute (not classList/dataset)
8. The server is running on port 8080 and serving the file correctly

All API calls work correctly as verified by curl tests.",
  "plan": "The task is complete. The app.js has been written with all required functionality and is being served correctly over HTTP on port 8080.",
  "commands": [],
  "task_complete": true
}

Current terminal state:
New Terminal Output:

{"id":144,"title":"Test","content":"Body"}root@78f42a19e0d4:/app#


Are you sure you want to mark the task as complete? This will trigger your solution to be graded and you won't be able to make any further corrections. If so, include "task_complete": true in your JSON response again.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
The last piece is the browser client in /app/www/app.js. Point fetch at the same-origin API base /api/notes. When the script runs, load the list into the notes-list element. Each row needs edit and delete buttons whose class lists include edit-btn and delete-btn.

Submitting note-form should create a note when nothing is selected and update the active row otherwise. When create or save succeeds, note-title and note-content should be empty and the form should be ready for a new note on the next submit rather than staying on the previous row. Delete must call the API and refresh the list. After every successful POST, PUT, or DELETE, reload the list so the page matches the database.

Send Content-Type application/json and the trimmed API key from /app/config/api.key on POST, PUT, and DELETE only, not on GET. The automated frontend harness is not a real browser; read /app/docs/js_verifier_mock.md for DOM and fetch quirks before you declare done. Keep /app.js reachable over HTTP from the running server on port 8080, not only on disk.

Automated frontend checks load /app/www/app.js in a minimal QuickJS DOM mock, not a full browser. The mock is incomplete compared to Chrome.

Do not use element.dataset or element.classList; use getAttribute and setAttribute for data attributes, and className (or split className on spaces) for classes
. After HTML is rendered into notes-list, elements inside that container do not expose parentNode, closest, querySelector, or getElementsByTagName. Edit and del
ete handlers must read note id and any other needed fields from getAttribute on the clicked edit-btn or delete-btn. Per-button click listeners that close over t
he note object are fine.

Load the note list when the script first runs (top-level code), not only after window load or DOMContentLoaded. Use class names edit-btn and delete-btn on row b
uttons. Hook form submit with addEventListener on the element with id note-form. For edit and delete, attach per-button click listeners, or delegate clicks on n
otes-list or document only if the handler reads note data from the clicked button itself; relying only on window load handlers will not exercise the script.

Mutating fetch calls must send Content-Type: application/json even for DELETE. Form submissions must send JSON bodies built from the note-title and note-content
 field values with keys title and content, not empty or hard-coded placeholders.

The harness seeds GET /api/notes with two notes: title Note One with content Body One, and title Note Two with content Body Two. Other strings used during check
s are arbitrary labels for form fields or API bodies only, including Find Me, In the List, Single Fetch, Updated Name, Updated Title, Echo Title, Echo Body, Edi
ted body, E2E Create, and Note B Updated. Snapshots may expose listChildren as a read-only rendered-row count.

Verifier-side Python harness code for this app may import urllib for HTTP probes, signal for subprocess timing, and quickjs to execute app.js in the mock DOM.

The mock fetch implementation exposes json() on responses; prefer json() over text() for parsing bodies. Async patterns must complete within the mock job loop s
o post-mutation refreshes and form clearing actually run.
